---
title: "GitHub workflow"
---

## Complete the GitHub workflow

This is the final assignment for Module 3. By this point you should have:

- Cloned your `md-03-USERNAME` repository to Posit Cloud ([Assignment 1](am-03-3-github-clone.qmd))
- Worked through the case study ([Assignment 2](am-03-2-case-study.qmd)) and written your answers into [`hw-data-stewardship.qmd`]{.highlight-yellow} in your Posit Cloud project
- Completed the learning reflection in [`learning-03.qmd`]{.highlight-yellow} in your Posit Cloud project

Now you will commit and push those changes to GitHub.

::: {.callout-note}
You already configured git as part of the Module 2 in-class session. Git configuration is a one-time setup; you do not need to repeat it for this or future homework repositories. Your workflow for every module from here on is just the commit and push steps below.
:::

## Commit and push your changes

1. In Posit Cloud, open the [md-03-USERNAME]{.highlight-yellow} project. Make sure you are in the [{{< var course.code >}}]{.highlight-yellow} workspace (check the top left corner; if it says [Your Workspace]{.highlight-yellow} you are in the wrong place, go back to [Assignment 1](am-03-3-github-clone.qmd) and clone again into the correct workspace).

2. Confirm both files have been saved with your work:

    - `hw-data-stewardship.qmd` contains your written critique answers
    - `learning-03.qmd` contains your learning reflection

3. Render each file (click the [Render]{.highlight-yellow} button in the Source pane) and check it produces an HTML output without errors.

4. Open the Git pane in the top-right window of RStudio.

5. Stage your changes by checking the boxes next to the changed files (`hw-data-stewardship.qmd`, `learning-03.qmd`, and any rendered output files).

6. Click the [Commit]{.highlight-yellow} button. A new window opens.

7. Enter a meaningful commit message, for example: [completed week 3 data stewardship homework and learning reflection]{.highlight-yellow}.

8. Click [Commit]{.highlight-yellow}. You should see a confirmation message. Click [Close]{.highlight-yellow}.

9. Click the [Push]{.highlight-yellow} button in the top right corner.

10. When prompted, enter your GitHub username and click OK.

11. When prompted, enter your GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) and click OK. This is the PAT you created in [Module 1, Assignment 4](../md-01/am-01-4-github-pat.qmd).

If you see a message that ends with `HEAD -> main`, you have successfully pushed your changes to the remote repository on GitHub. Click [Close]{.highlight-yellow}.

::: {.callout-warning}
## PAT Storage Warning

If you have stored your PAT in a Word file, a blank space might have been copied along with the PAT. We recommend using a TXT file (Notepad on Windows or TextEdit on Mac) or a password manager to store your PAT securely.
:::

## Verify on GitHub

1. Navigate to your `md-03-USERNAME` repository at [{{< var course.github-org >}}]({{< var course.github-org >}}) and refresh the page.

2. You should see your commit message next to the updated files, and the changes you made in `hw-data-stewardship.qmd` and `learning-03.qmd` should be visible on GitHub.

You have now completed the GitHub workflow for Module 3.
